preamble: The diphthong [aɪ] reduces to schwa [ə] in unstressed syllables, a stress-conditioned vowel reduction process common in Germanic languages, particularly English. This shift reflects the phonetic economy of unstressed positions, where full vowel articulation is reduced to a centralized schwa.

examples.note: The pronoun 'my' shows vowel reduction in unstressed contexts, as in 'my house' where the diphthong reduces to schwa in casual speech.
The reduced form of 'day' in unstressed syllables, particularly visible in compound formations or weakly stressed positions.
Reduction of the diphthong [aɪ] to schwa occurs in unstressed word-final positions in rapid or connected speech.
